title: Protocol for Making Soil
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2qr9l","text":"This protocol outlines 3 soil recipes for their corresponding plant species: Phacelia parryi, P. campanularia, and Brassica rapa. In addition to the recipes, it also details the soil mixing process.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":77,"length":34},{"style":"italic","offset":115,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"dj353","text":"Peat moss","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9pph5","text":"Vermiculite","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f3s97","text":"Perlite","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6nj7a","text":"Sand","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c2g85","text":"Fertilizer (Osmocote Flower \u0026 Vegetable)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cllug","text":"Water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3gcqe","text":"Mixing shovel","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6fg02","text":"Clear food grade bucket ~2 gallons for mixing soil","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 1 part peat moss 0.5 part vermiculite 1.5 part sand 3 parts perlite 3 tbsp of fertilizer per 2 gallons of soil
1 part peat moss 1 part vermiculite 1 part perlite 3 tbsp of fertilizer per 2 gallons of soil
1 part peat moss 1 part vermiculite 1 part sand 2 part perlite 3 tbsp of fertilizer per 2 gallons of soil
Depending on what plant you desire to plant, follow the corresponding soil recipe to measure all required parts except perlite and fertilizer and place all parts within a sterile container.
Using gloved hands or a shovel thoroughly mix the soil until there are not hidden pockets of unmixed material.
If the recipe calls for perlite, add the perlite parts to the container and rinse with DI water to minimize dust. Once wet, repeat step 5.
Depending on the total number of gallons of soil produced, measure out the correct amount of fertilizer and add it to the soil container. Then, repeat step 5.
Once evenly mixed, your soil is now ready to be used for seed planting and transplanting.
creator: Yuriani Palomino
